prompt: I need to write a Node.js script that connects to the Blender MCP server on port 9876, creates a simple table (a flat top surface with 4 legs), exports it as STL and FBX, renders a hero shot and a top-down shot, then saves the .blend file. The script should handle scene cleanup at the start, apply subdivision surface and smooth shading, and set up proper lighting. Write the complete script.

assertions.text: MCP calls use command names without blender_ prefix
All Python code inside JS strings uses single quotes only
Socket data handler uses brace-depth counting, not readline
Scene cleanup uses save_file with use_empty AND Python nuke
Shade smooth uses execute_python, NOT blender_cleanup
STL export uses execute_python with bpy.ops.wm.stl_export()
Render calls use output_path parameter, not filepath
World background is gray (~0.18), not white
Camera position calculated from model bounding box
